leg: qwen3-30b seq512 shape verdict rules (ADDENDUM A1+A4)
arm_state_at_commit: q512_routed ARM_START 11:44:32Z, still running at 12:12:02Z (SENT metadata only read; no rf probe or eval output opened)

frozen_fits_through_the_two_anchors.separation: peaks ~0.017 (~17x floor) around fill 12-15%; collapses below ~6x floor outside fill ~6-25% (rf ~0.75-0.94)

verdict_rule: 1. compute both point-predictions at the MEASURED rf (not nominal, not predicted)
2. verdict = the model whose prediction the measured gap lands closer to, ONLY IF |d_power - d_affine| >= 3x floor (floor = 0.0010, today's bracket)
3. otherwise the recorded verdict is exactly 'shape underdetermined at this fill point' — no leaning language, no 'consistent with', no directional gloss
4. if measured fill lands outside 6-25%, record 'outside discriminating zone' and apply rule 3 regardless of proximity
comparator_status_A2: the affine comparator is already physically non-viable at the boundary: forward bit-identity forces gap(fill=0) ~= floor ~= 0.001, and the affine intercept is 0.049 (~50x violation of a free constraint); linear-through-origin cannot fit both anchors (implied slopes 2.07 vs 0.60). Today is a THIRD-POINT CONSISTENCY TEST of the power law against a non-viable comparator, NOT a two-model horse race. Mid-zone power-side language: 'power law holds at a third point', NOT 'power beat linear'.
registered_prediction_for_fix_acceptance: k ~ 0.47 ~ 0.5 is the quadrature signature: per-filled-row perturbations acting as independent noise injections (variances add -> gap ~ sqrt(fill)); coherent systematic bias would scale linearly. PREDICTION (for the mask-fix acceptance run, NOT tested today): if the corruption is incoherent noise, the gap should ATTENUATE under gradient averaging (larger batch or more accumulation steps at fixed fill); coherent bias would NOT attenuate. Committed now so it predates any fix work.
